session.user:
Add `curated_in: erk-tui` to the YAML frontmatter of the following docs/learned/tui/ files. The field should be added as the LAST field before the closing `---` in each file's frontmatter block.
For each file: 1. Read the file to see its current frontmatter 2. Find the closing `---` of the frontmatter block 3. Add `curated_in: erk-tui` as a new line just before the closing `---`
Files to edit (all in /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/): 1. multi-operation-tracking.md 2. one-shot-prompt-modal.md 3. plan-row-data.md 4. plan-title-rendering-pipeline.md 5. runs-tab-architecture.md 6. stacked-pr-indicator.md 7. status-indicators.md 8. streaming-output.md 9. subprocess-feedback.md 10. textual-async.md
For example, if a file has: ``` --- title: Some Title read_when: - "some condition" last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" audit_result: edited --- ```
It should become: ``` --- title: Some Title read_when: - "some condition" last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" audit_result: edited curated_in: erk-tui --- ```
IMPORTANT: The `curated_in: erk-tui` line goes after ALL existing fields, immediately before the closing `---`.
Do NOT modify any content after the frontmatter block.
session.assistant: I'll add `curated_in: erk-tui` to the frontmatter of each file in the docs/learned/tui/ directory. Let me start by reading all the files to see their current structure.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: TUI Multi-Operation Tracking 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new background operation to the TUI" 5→ - "implementing status bar progress for a workflow command" 6→ - "debugging operation lifecycle or stuck operations" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "calling start_operation() without a matching finish_operation() in the error path" 9→ warning: "Always call finish_operation() in both success and error paths. Use try/finally or explicit error handling. Missing finish calls leave ghost operations in the status bar." 10→ - action: "constructing op IDs without the {action}-{resource}-{id} pattern" 11→ warning: "Op IDs must follow the convention: f'{action}-{resource_type}-{resource_id}' (e.g., 'land-pr-456', 'close-plan-123'). Non-unique IDs cause operations to overwrite each other." 12→ - action: "using subprocess.run() instead of _run_streaming_operation() for TUI commands" 13→ warning: "TUI background operations must use _run_streaming_operation() for live progress. subprocess.run() blocks without streaming and produces no status bar updates." 14→ - action: "calling self.notify() or self._finish_operation() directly from a background thread" 15→ warning: "Use self.call_from_thread() for all UI updates from @work(thread=True) methods. Direct calls cause thread-safety violations." 16→--- 17→ 18→# TUI Multi-Operation Tracking 19→ 20→The TUI supports multiple concurrent background operations with live progress display in the status bar. Operations are tracked via a registry pattern with start/update/finish lifecycle methods. 21→ 22→## Architecture 23→ 24→### \_OperationState 25→ 26→Frozen dataclass in `src/erk/tui/widgets/status_bar.py`. Stores the current label and progress text for a single operation. See source file for implementation details. 27→ 28→### Operations Registry 29→ 30→`_operations: dict[str, _OperationState]` in `src/erk/tui/widgets/status_bar.py` maps op IDs to their current state. Supports multiple concurrent operations; the most recently updated one is displayed.
... [59 lines omitted] ...
72→**Error path:** Extract the last line of output, finish the operation, and display an error toast with `severity="error"` and `timeout=5` for longer visibility.

1→--- 2→title: One-Shot Prompt Modal 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new modal or input screen to the TUI" 5→ - "working with the one-shot dispatch workflow in erk dash" 6→ - "adding global keybindings that trigger modals" 7→--- 8→ 9→# One-Shot Prompt Modal 10→ 11→The `x` keybinding in erk dash opens a modal for dispatching one-shot prompts. This is a global action — no row selection is required. 12→ 13→## Screen Implementation 14→ 15→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/screens/one_shot_prompt_screen.py --> 16→ 17→`OneShotPromptScreen(ModalScreen[str | None])` at `src/erk/tui/screens/one_shot_prompt_screen.py`: 18→ 19→- **Widget**: `TextArea` (not `Input`) for multi-line prompt support, 32-line height 20→- **Submit**: Ctrl+Enter dispatches the prompt (returns stripped text) 21→- **Cancel**: Escape dismisses (returns `None`) 22→- **No `q` binding**: The screen deliberately omits a `q` keybinding since users need to type `q` in prompts 23→ 24→### Design Decision: TextArea vs Input 25→ 26→`TextArea` was chosen over `Input` to allow multi-line prompts. One-shot prompts often contain detailed instructions spanning multiple lines. The 32-line height provides ample space without overwhelming the screen. 27→ 28→### Design Decision: Ctrl+Enter vs Enter 29→ 30→Ctrl+Enter (not Enter) submits because Enter inserts newlines in the `TextArea`. This avoids interfering with multi-line editing.
... [39 lines omitted] ...
55→- **Failure**: Shows error toast with the last output line

1→--- 2→title: PlanRowData Field Reference 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing command availability predicates" 5→ - "understanding what data is available for TUI commands" 6→ - "checking which PlanRowData fields are nullable" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-23 00:15 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→--- 10→ 11→# PlanRowData Field Reference 12→ 13→Quick reference for writing command availability predicates and understanding 14→`PlanRowData` usage patterns. 15→ 16→## Overview 17→ 18→`PlanRowData` is a frozen dataclass containing all data for a single plan row in the TUI. It combines raw data (for actions) with pre-formatted display strings (for table rendering). 19→ 20→**Location:** `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` 21→ 22→## Field Categories 23→ 24→### Plan Info 25→ 26→| Field | Type | Description | Nullable? | 27→| ------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------- | 28→| `plan_id` | `int` | Plan identifier | Never | 29→| `plan_url` | `str \| None` | Full URL to the plan | Yes | 30→| `full_title` | `str` | Complete plan title | Never (empty string possible) |
... [196 lines omitted] ...
80→| `run_conclusion` | `str \| None` | Run conclusion: "success", "failure", "cancelled" | Yes |

1→--- 2→title: TUI Plan Title Rendering Pipeline 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 04:53 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "debugging why plan titles display incorrectly" 7→ - "troubleshooting missing prefixes in TUI" 8→ - "understanding plan data flow in TUI" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "modifying how plan titles are displayed in TUI" 11→ warning: "Ensure `[erk-learn]` prefix is added BEFORE any filtering/sorting stages." 12→ - action: "using title-stripping functions" 13→ warning: "Distinguish `_strip_plan_prefixes` (PR creation) vs `_strip_plan_markers` (plan creation) vs `strip_plan_from_filename` (filename handling)." 14→--- 15→ 16→# TUI Plan Title Rendering Pipeline 17→ 18→Plan titles pass through multiple stages before appearing in the TUI. Issues can occur at any stage. 19→ 20→## The 5-Stage Pipeline 21→ 22→``` 23→GitHub API → Middleware (prefix) → Filtering → Service (transform) → Widget (render) 24→``` 25→ 26→### Stage 1: GitHub API Response 27→ 28→GitHub returns raw issue/PR data: 29→ 30→```json
... [100 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: "Runs Tab Architecture" 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with TUI Runs tab" 5→ - "adding columns to RunDataTable" 6→ - "understanding branch resolution in runs" 7→ - "modifying workflow run display in TUI" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "using run.branch directly for display without checking PR head_branch first" 10→ warning: "After merge+deletion, run.branch becomes master/main. Use PR head_branch as primary source, falling back to run.branch only if not master/main." 11→ - action: "adding a new column to RunDataTable without updating RunRowData" 12→ warning: "RunDataTable columns are populated from RunRowData fields. Add the field to RunRowData first, then add the column in _setup_columns." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Runs Tab Architecture 16→ 17→The Runs tab displays GitHub Actions workflow runs in the TUI, alongside Plans, Learn, and Objectives views. 18→ 19→## RunRowData 20→ 21→Frozen dataclass representing one workflow run row. 22→ 23→**Source**: `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` 24→ 25→| Field | Type | Description | 26→| ------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | 27→| `run_id` | `str` | GitHub Actions workflow run ID | 28→| `run_url` | `str \| None` | URL to the GitHub Actions run page | 29→| `status` | `str` | Raw status: "queued", "in_progress", "completed" | 30→| `conclusion` | `str \| None` | Raw conclusion: "success", "failure", "cancelled", None |
... [78 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Stacked PR Indicator 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding or modifying PR indicators in the TUI dashboard" 5→ - "understanding blocking vs. informational indicators" 6→ - "working with stacked PR detection" 7→--- 8→ 9→# Stacked PR Indicator 10→ 11→The pancake emoji (🥞) indicates a stacked PR in the TUI dashboard lifecycle column. It appears when a plan's PR targets a branch other than master/main. 12→ 13→## Dual-Source Detection 14→ 15→Stacked status is detected from two sources with a fallback strategy: 16→ 17→1. **Primary: Graphite `get_parent_branch()`** — When the PR head branch is known, the branch manager's `get_parent_branch()` is consulted first. If it returns a parent branch not in `("master", "main")`, the PR is stacked. Graphite is authoritative because it reflects the actual stack topology. 18→ 19→2. **Fallback: GitHub `base_ref_name`** — Only when Graphite returns `None` (branch not tracked locally). The `base_ref_name` field on `PullRequestInfo` is checked against `("master", "main")`. 20→ 21→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py, RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data --> 22→ 23→See `RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data()` in `real.py` for the dual-source detection logic. 24→ 25→## Stale Data Handling 26→ 27→Graphite is authoritative because GitHub's `base_ref_name` can become stale. When a parent PR merges, GitHub does not always update the child PR's target branch immediately — the child may still show the old (now-merged) branch as its base. Graphite's local branch tracking reflects the actual stack state, making it the more reliable source for stacking detection. 28→ 29→## Indicator Ordering 30→
... [42 lines omitted] ...
41→- **Blocking indicators** (CI failures, unresolved reviews, merge conflicts) prevent the rocket emoji (🚀) from appearing in the implemented stage.

1→--- 2→title: TUI Status Indicators 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with status indicators in TUI dashboard" 5→ - "adding new emoji indicators to plan display" 6→ - "modifying the sts column" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding emoji with Unicode variation selector (\\ufe0f) to status indicators" 9→ warning: "Variation selector forces double-wide rendering in terminals, breaking column alignment. Current safe emoji: 🥞 🚧 👀 💥 ✔ ❌ 🚀. Test any new emoji in terminal before adding." 10→ score: 5 11→ - action: "extracting status indicators from the lifecycle display string" 12→ warning: "Indicators are computed from RAW PR state fields (is_draft, has_conflicts, review_decision), NOT extracted from lifecycle display. Use compute_status_indicators() for standalone display, format_lifecycle_with_status() for inline." 13→ score: 4 14→--- 15→ 16→# TUI Status Indicators 17→ 18→Status indicators show PR state as emoji in the TUI dashboard. They are computed from raw PR state fields, not extracted from the lifecycle display string. 19→ 20→## Two Public Functions 21→ 22→**Location:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py` 23→ 24→### compute_status_indicators() — Standalone Display 25→ 26→Used for the "sts" column in the dashboard. Returns space-joined emoji string or "-" when empty. 27→ 28→```python 29→compute_status_indicators( 30→ lifecycle_display="[cyan]impl[/cyan]",
... [61 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Streaming Output Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "displaying streaming command output in TUI" 5→ - "executing long-running commands with progress" 6→ - "cross-thread UI updates in Textual" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-05 00:00 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "accessing _status_bar without null guard" 11→ warning: "Guard _status_bar access with `if self._status_bar is not None:` — timing issue during widget lifecycle can cause AttributeError." 12→ - action: "using subprocess.Popen without bufsize=1 for streaming" 13→ warning: "Use bufsize=1 with text=True for line-buffered streaming Popen output. Without it, output may be block-buffered." 14→ - action: "displaying subprocess output in plain text widgets without stripping ANSI" 15→ warning: "Use click.unstyle() before displaying subprocess output in plain text widgets. Raw ANSI codes render as garbage." 16→--- 17→ 18→# TUI Streaming Output Patterns 19→ 20→Patterns for displaying streaming command output in the Erk TUI without blocking the UI thread. 21→ 22→For subprocess execution strategies (streaming vs executor, `stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL` requirement), see [Command Execution Strategies](command-execution.md). 23→ 24→## Architecture Components 25→ 26→1. **Background Thread** - Reads subprocess output via `subprocess.PIPE` 27→2. **Output Widget** - `CommandOutputPanel` (`src/erk/tui/widgets/command_output.py`) displays streaming content 28→3. **Cross-Thread Bridge** - `app.call_from_thread()` safely updates UI from background thread 29→ 30→## Critical: Threading Safety with `call_from_thread`
... [93 lines omitted] ...
81→4. **Output collection**: Lines stored in a list for error context extraction on failure 82→5. **Last non-empty line extraction**: On failure, extract the last non-empty line from output for error messages 105→ msg = next((ln for ln in reversed(output_lines) if ln), "Unknown error") 106→ self.call_from_thread(self.notify, msg, severity="error")

1→--- 2→title: TUI Subprocess Feedback Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding subprocess calls to the TUI" 5→ - "implementing background worker feedback in the TUI" 6→ - "parsing subprocess stderr for status markers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a subprocess call to the TUI without stderr inspection" 9→ warning: "TUI subprocess calls should inspect stderr for known success/failure markers. See subprocess-feedback.md for the pattern." 10→--- 11→ 12→# TUI Subprocess Feedback Patterns 13→ 14→The TUI executes subprocess commands in background workers and provides feedback to users via `self.notify()`. A consistent pattern is used across all subprocess-calling methods. 15→ 16→## Subprocess Error Extraction 17→ 18→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/app.py --> 19→ 20→A helper function extracts human-readable error messages from the operation result object: 21→ 22→1. Iterate backwards through the output lines (merged stdout/stderr) 23→2. Return the first non-empty line found 24→3. Fall back to `"Unknown error"` if all lines are empty 25→ 26→## Stderr Inspection for Status Markers 27→ 28→TUI background workers check `result.stderr` for specific success markers after subprocess calls. For example, the address-remote handler checks for `"Updated dispatch metadata"` in stderr to confirm a workflow dispatch succeeded. 29→ 30→This pattern is necessary because subprocess exit code 0 alone doesn't distinguish between "successfully dispatched" and "silently did nothing."
... [26 lines omitted] ...
38→3. On failure (`return_code != 0`): extract the last non-empty output line as the error message, notify with `severity="error"` (5s timeout) 45→CLI-side diagnostic output via `user_output` warnings was implemented in PR #8078 but subsequently reverted in commit a50fa13f2 (which eliminated the `--no-wait` flag and `dispatch_workflow()` method). The silent failure problem — where a dispatch succeeds but produces no user-visible feedback — remains a known gap. The TUI-side stderr inspection partially mitigates this, but CLI users don't benefit from it. 50→- [Error Detection Patterns](../cli/error-detection-patterns.md) — Keyword detection for parsing subprocess stderr

1→--- 2→title: Textual Async Best Practices 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "overriding Screen actions" 7→ - "working with async/await in Textual" 8→ - "testing async TUI code" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Textual Async Best Practices 12→ 13→Guide for working with async/await in Textual applications. 14→ 15→## When to Make Actions Async 16→ 17→Actions (methods prefixed with `action_`) can be either sync or async depending on what they do: 18→ 19→**Sync Actions** - For immediate, non-blocking operations: 20→ 21→```python 22→def action_toggle_filter(self) -> None: 23→ """Toggle filter visibility.""" 24→ self._filter_visible = not self._filter_visible 25→ self.query_one("#filter").visible = self._filter_visible 26→``` 27→ 28→**Async Actions** - When you need to: 29→ 30→- Wait for I/O operations
... [147 lines omitted] ...
53→ super().action_dismiss() # Type error! 129→def action_submit(self) -> None: # Type error 152→## Exception Handling in Background Workers (Error Boundary Pattern) 154→When using `@work(thread=True)` for background data fetching, exceptions in the worker thread are swallowed by Textual unless explicitly handled. The approved pattern wraps the entire worker body in a try/except that calls `self.app.call_from_thread()` to report errors back to the UI thread: 162→ except Exception as e: 163→ self.app.call_from_thread(self._on_error, str(e)) 166→This is an **approved EAFP exception** for UI error boundaries — the try/except is not for control flow but for preventing silent worker failures. The `call_from_thread()` bridge ensures widget mutations happen on the UI thread.

session.assistant:
Perfect! I've successfully added `curated_in: erk-tui` to the YAML frontmatter of all 10 files in the `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/` directory.
## Summary
All files have been updated with the `curated_in: erk-tui` field added as the last field before the closing `---` in the frontmatter:
1. **multi-operation-tracking.md** - Added after tripwires section 2. **one-shot-prompt-modal.md** - Added after read_when section 3. **plan-row-data.md** - Added after audit_result field 4. **plan-title-rendering-pipeline.md** - Added after tripwires section 5. **runs-tab-architecture.md** - Added after tripwires section 6. **stacked-pr-indicator.md** - Added after read_when section 7. **status-indicators.md** - Added after tripwires section 8. **streaming-output.md** - Added after tripwires section 9. **subprocess-feedback.md** - Added after tripwires section 10. **textual-async.md** - Added after read_when section
The field was placed immediately before the closing `---` as requested, without modifying any content after the frontmatter block.
